county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1813 from Ste. Genevieve. County seat: Potosi. French colonial heritage — area settled under French and Spanish governance before Louisiana Purchase (1803). Missouri Lead Mining District — lead mining from French colonial era to present.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Potosi holds land records from 1813. Washington County has exceptional early mining heritage — Potosi (named for Potosi, Bolivia) was the center of early lead mining under both French and American governance. Moses Austin began lead mining here in 1798 under Spanish authority. Washington County Historical Society has genealogical files. French and Spanish colonial land grants at Missouri State Archives. Significant German immigration to mining communities. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Check Ste. Genevieve for pre-1813 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Missouri State Archives microfilm and digitized records for Washington from colonial era

source_profiles.source_name: Washington County Deed Records -- Recorder of Deeds, Potosi
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1813 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Washington County Probate Records -- Probate Court, Potosi
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, administrations, and guardianship records from 1813. Missouri probate courts also handle some adoption records.

source_profiles.source_name: Washington County Marriage Records -- Recorder of Deeds / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records from 1813. Missouri marriage records kept at county level by Recorder of Deeds. FamilySearch has indexed many Missouri marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Washington County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1820-1950 for Washington. Missouri territorial census 1820; state census 1844, 1852, 1856, 1860, 1864, 1868, 1876 (incomplete). Slave schedules 1850, 1860.

source_profiles.source_name: Washington County Original Land Entry Records -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Washington County. Missouri is a public land state — BLM GLO records document first transfer of land from federal government. Some areas have earlier French/Spanish colonial grants.

source_profiles.source_name: French and Spanish Colonial Court Records -- Washington Area
source_profiles.repository: Missouri State Archives, Jefferson City / Ste. Genevieve Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: French colonial court records, Spanish colonial land grants, parish registers, and administrative records for the Washington area. Louisiana Purchase (1803) transferred governance to the US.

source_profiles.source_name: Lead Mining District Land and Property Records -- Washington County
source_profiles.repository: Missouri Mines State Historic Site / Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mining claims, land patents, mining company property records, and associated community records for Washington County's lead mining operations. Missouri's Lead Belt was the nation's primary lead producer from colonial times.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1818 from Cape Girardeau, Lawrence (extinct). County seat: Greenville. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Greenville holds records restarting after the 1854 courthouse fire that destroyed many early records. For pre-1854 research, check Cape Girardeau County (parent) and Missouri State Archives. Wayne County is in the southeastern Ozarks with significant timber and iron mining history. Wappapello Lake (1941) flooded communities — check Corps of Engineers relocation records. Wayne County Historical Society has post-fire genealogical files. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-fire records. Strong Southern settlement heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1818

source_profiles.source_name: Wayne County Deed Records -- Recorder of Deeds, Greenville
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1818 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Wayne County Probate Records -- Probate Court, Greenville
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, administrations, and guardianship records from 1818. Missouri probate courts also handle some adoption records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records from 1818. Missouri marriage records kept at county level by Recorder of Deeds. FamilySearch has indexed many Missouri marriages.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1820-1950 for Wayne. Missouri territorial census 1820; state census 1844, 1852, 1856, 1860, 1864, 1868, 1876 (incomplete). Slave schedules 1850, 1860.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Wayne County. Missouri is a public land state — BLM GLO records document first transfer of land from federal government. Some areas have earlier French/Spanish colonial grants.

source_profiles.source_name: Lead Mining District Land and Property Records -- Wayne County
source_profiles.repository: Missouri Mines State Historic Site / Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mining claims, land patents, mining company property records, and associated community records for Wayne County's lead mining operations. Missouri's Lead Belt was the nation's primary lead producer from colonial times.

source_profiles.source_name: Wayne County Tax Records -- Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists and personal property assessments from 1818. List heads of household, property, livestock, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Useful as census substitutes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Generally well preserved at Missouri State Archives

county_metadata.record_losses: 1880 courthouse fire destroyed many records; 1958 fire caused additional losses
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1855 from Greene. County seat: Marshfield. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement. Burned county: 1880 courthouse fire destroyed many records; 1958 fire caused additional losses
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Marshfield holds records with gaps due to the 1880 courthouse fire and the 1958 fire that caused additional losses. For pre-1880 research, check Greene County (parent) and Missouri State Archives. Webster County Historical Society in Marshfield has genealogical files. Marshfield was devastated by a tornado in 1880 (same year as fire). Ozark heritage with Southern settlement patterns. Route 66 passed through the county. FamilySearch microfilm covers available records. Missouri State Archives may have surviving fragments.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1855

source_profiles.source_name: Webster County Deed Records -- Recorder of Deeds, Marshfield
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1880 to present. Post-destruction records; pre-1880 records largely lost.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1880 courthouse fire destroyed many records; 1958 fire caused additional losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1861 from Gentry. County seat: Grant City.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Grant City holds land records from 1861. Worth County is Missouri's least populous county. Worth County Historical Society has limited genealogical files. The county was created just as the Civil War began — formed from Gentry County. Northwest Missouri agricultural community. Check Gentry County for pre-1861 records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. Small population means church records and cemetery records are critical.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1861

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1841 from Pulaski. County seat: Hartville. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Hartville holds records restarting after Civil War courthouse destruction. For pre-war records, check Pulaski County (parent), Missouri State Archives, and BLM GLO land patents. Wright County is in the Ozark Highlands. Wright County Historical Society in Hartville has post-war genealogical files. Severe guerrilla warfare during the Civil War devastated the county. Strong Southern Appalachian settlement heritage. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-war records. Missouri State Archives holds some surviving circuit court records. Church records are essential supplements.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1841
